name: getSubAgent
description:
Spawns an isolated AI subagent to handle a task using a dedicated tool palette. Returns a single synthesized result.

CONTEXT SCAN BEFORE EVERY CALL: Before writing the task field, scan the recent conversation for any artifact URLs, ARTIFACTS blocks, file URLs, image URLs, or ZIP URLs produced by previous subagent calls. Pass all relevant ones into the task — subagents cannot see the conversation history and will regenerate everything from scratch if you omit them.

DIRECT TOOLS FIRST: Use available tools directly for simple tasks (getTavily for web search, getImage for a single standalone image). Only use getSubAgent when the task requires tools not available here, or is multi-step. NEVER write code inline in your response — any coding task must go through getSubAgent(type: 'develop').

ONE CALL PER DELIVERABLE: Each subagent call produces one output. Do not split a single deliverable across multiple calls.

SEQUENTIAL DEPENDENCIES: If subagent B needs the result of subagent A, call A first, wait for its result, then pass it explicitly in B's task — never let B discover the dependency itself.

Available types:
- 'generate': PDF and document assembly. Self-contained: generates any required portrait internally. Describe the full document including portrait requirements. Call at most ONCE per request.
- 'media': Image generation, animation, video, and token creation. Pass all sequential steps in ONE task (e.g. 'generate portrait → animate → create token'). Never split media work across multiple calls. The response always contains an ARTIFACTS block with all produced URLs (portrait, animation, token, video). IMPORTANT: For follow-up requests (e.g. 'token in different color'), check your context for the ARTIFACTS block from the previous media response — extract those URLs and include them labeled in the new task (e.g. 'portrait: https://...', 'animation: https://...'). Never ask the user for URLs that are already in your context. The subagent will reuse the provided artifacts and only redo the requested step.
- 'history': Search session logs for facts about persons, places, or events.
- 'research': Web search, webpage fetch, YouTube — for current events and general knowledge requiring live data.
- 'develop': Use for ANY request to write, build, fix, modify, or extend code or software — regardless of size or complexity. Never write code inline. IMPORTANT: The subagent has no access to conversation history. You MUST include everything it needs in the task field: the full request AND all existing file URLs that are already in your context. Never ask the user for URLs that you already have. For modifications, develop delegates per-file edits to 'patch' subagents automatically.
- 'patch': Reads ONE existing file by URL, applies ONE targeted change, and returns the new file URL. Only callable from within a 'develop' subagent. Pass the file URL and the exact change description.
- 'atlassian': Jira and Confluence.
- 'microsoft': Microsoft 365 / Graph API.
- 'orchestrate': Only when the request has truly independent parts requiring different tool sets simultaneously.

task.description: Complete, self-contained task description. The subagent has NO access to conversation history — everything it needs must be in this field. ALWAYS scan the recent conversation before writing this field: look for ARTIFACTS blocks, file URLs, image URLs, or ZIP URLs from previous subagent responses. Include all relevant previously-produced artifacts in the task so the subagent can reuse them instead of regenerating. Never ask the user for URLs or files that are already in your context.

type.description: Subagent type determining which tool palette is used.

orchestration.description: Optional orchestration context that scopes this subagent's work. Prevents duplicate side effects when multiple subagents are coordinated. Schema: { global_goal, your_task, your_role, do_only[], do_not[], existing_artifacts{}, assigned_to_others[], tool_locks{} }. Pass this whenever more than one subagent is involved in fulfilling a single user request.

global_goal.description: The overall user request this subagent is part of.

your_task.description: Exact deliverable this subagent must produce.

do_only.description: Exhaustive list of what this subagent is allowed to do.

do_not.description: Explicit prohibitions — actions this subagent must not take.

existing_artifacts.description: Artifacts already produced. Keys are artifact types (e.g. 'portrait_image'), values are URLs or data.

assigned_to_others.description: Tasks already assigned to other subagents. This subagent must not redo them.

tool_locks.description: Tools this subagent must not call. Keys are tool names, values are the reason (e.g. 'already generated by media agent').

channel_id.description: Optional: explicit channel ID overriding the type-based channel lookup.
